title: Aliases
description: Learn how to create aliases to automate and simplify your commands

sections.title: What Are Aliases?
sections.content:
Aliases transform commands you type before they are sent to the MUD server. They allow you to:

- Create shortcuts for long commands
- Add automatic arguments
- Chain multiple commands together

For example, typing 'l' could automatically become 'look'.

sections.title: Alias Types
sections.content:
MUDPuppy supports two types of aliases:

**Exact Match**
- The command must match the pattern exactly
- Example: Pattern 'l' matches only 'l', not 'look'

**Prefix Match**
- The command must start with the pattern
- Example: Pattern 'k' matches 'k', 'kill', 'kick', etc.

Choose the type that fits your needs.

sections.title: The {args} Placeholder
sections.content:
Use {args} in your replacement text to include any additional text you type:

| Pattern | Type | Replacement | You Type | Sends |
|---------|------|-------------|----------|-------|
| l | exact | look | l | look |
| k | prefix | kill {args} | k goblin | kill goblin |
| cast | prefix | cast {args} | cast fireball | cast fireball |

{args} captures everything after your pattern.

sections.title: Command Chaining
sections.content:
You can send multiple commands by separating them with a semicolon (;):

Example:
- Pattern: 'k'
- Type: prefix
- Replacement: kill {args};stance offensive

When you type 'k goblin', it sends:
'kill goblin;stance offensive'

This executes both commands in sequence.

sections.title: Creating an Alias
sections.content:
To create an alias:

1. Go to Connection Settings
2. Navigate to Aliases
3. Click 'Add Alias'
4. Enter the pattern (what you'll type)
5. Select the type (exact or prefix)
6. Enter the replacement text
7. Click Save

Your alias is active immediately.

sections.title: Alias Priority
sections.content:
When you have multiple aliases that could match:

- Aliases are processed in order (top to bottom)
- The first matching alias is applied
- Only one alias transforms each command

Use the up/down arrows to reorder aliases and control which one takes priority.
